message:
refactor(viz): simplify axiom coherence spoke dots — static circle replaces trivial animation

Linter applied: the spoke endpoint circle animated radius from 2→2.6px,
which is imperceptible. Replaced motion.circle with plain circle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ch3","title":"Add embedded podcast players to Nvidia page","description":"# Task: Embedded Podcast Players

## Objective
Allow visitors to listen to podcast episodes about the Nvidia essay without leaving the page.

## Episodes to Embed

### 1. Bankless Podcast (Primary)
- Title: "DeepSeek R1 & The Short Case For Nvidia Stock"
- Link: https://www.bankless.com/podcast/deepseek-r1-the-short-case-for-nvidia-stock-jeffrey-emanuel
- Platform: Bankless (likely has embed option)
- Date: January 28, 2025

### 2. Delphi Digital
- Title: "Viral Author of The Short Case for Nvidia Stock"
- Link: https://members.delphidigital.io/media/jeffrey-emanuel-viral-author-of-the-short-case-for-nvidia-stock-cohosted-by-pondering-durian
- May be members-only (link instead of embed?)

### 3. Farzad Podcast
- Title: "Viral NVIDIA Short on Record Breaking $600B Loss"
- Link: https://open.spotify.com/episode/59UKRipYaHjsrQjEXHFR5z
- Platform: Spotify (has embed)

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Native Embeds
- Use each platform's embed iframe
- Pros: Native player experience, no extra dependencies
- Cons: Different visual styles, potential loading issues

### Option B: Just Links with Preview Cards
- Rich link cards with episode title, duration, platform logo
- Pros: Consistent styling, faster load, no third-party tracking
- Cons: Users leave the page to listen

### Option C: Hybrid
- One featured episode with embed (Bankless - most comprehensive)
- Others as link cards

## Recommended: Option C (Hybrid)
- Bankless episode gets Spotify/native embed
- Others get styled link cards with "Listen on Spotify" / "Listen on Delphi" buttons
- Lazy load embeds to avoid performance hit

## Accessibility
- Embeds should have proper aria-labels
- Alternative text links for those who can't use embeds

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Layout design task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:03:44.870815517Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T02:41:49.741390711Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T02:41:49.741390711Z","close_reason":"Created NvidiaPodcastSection component with lazy-loaded Spotify embeds, podcast cards with hover effects, featured/regular layouts. Component respects reduced motion and has loading/error states for embeds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ch3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-azv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:04:16.210090875Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cujd","title":"Evaluate WASM candidates for CPU-intensive operations","description":"# WASM Candidacy Evaluation

## Part of Feature
Performance Optimization Initiative

## Objective
Identify CPU-intensive operations that could benefit from Rust-to-WASM compilation for improved performance.

## Background
WebAssembly (WASM) can provide significant performance gains for:
- Heavy mathematical computations
- Data processing loops
- Cryptographic operations
- Image/video processing

However, WASM has overhead:
- ~10-100μs for JS↔WASM boundary crossing
- Additional bundle size (~100KB+ for Rust runtime)
- Increased build complexity

## Analysis Methodology

### 1. Profile CPU Usage
Examined all components for CPU-intensive patterns:
- Loops processing large datasets
- Complex mathematical calculations
- Recursive algorithms
- Real-time data processing

### 2. Candidates Evaluated

#### stats-grid.tsx: parseStatValue()
```tsx
function parseStatValue(value: string): { number: number; suffix: string; isAnimatable: boolean } {
  const match = value.match(/^([0-9,.]+)(K|M|B)?(\+)?$/i);
  // ... simple regex and string manipulation
}
```
**Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM**
- Execution time: <1μs per call
- Called: ~4 times total (for 4 stats)
- Total time: <4μs
- WASM overhead would exceed computation time

#### tldr-tool-grid.tsx: Fuse.js Search
```tsx
const fuse = new Fuse(tools, { ... });
const results = fuse.search(query);
```
**Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM**
- Already optimized by Fuse.js library
- Dataset: ~15 tools (small)
- Execution time: ~1-2ms
- WASM boundary crossing would negate benefits

#### three-scene.tsx: Star Generation
```tsx
for (let i = 0; i < numPoints; i++) {
  const r = 5 + rand() * 4;
  const theta = rand() * Math.PI * 2;
  // ... trigonometry
}
```
**Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM**
- Runs once during initialization
- numPoints: 100-400 depending on quality
- Execution time: <5ms
- Result is cached in useMemo

#### AnimatedNumber: Counter Animation
```tsx
const easeOutExpo = (t: number) => (t === 1 ? 1 : 1 - Math.pow(2, -10 * t));
setCount(easedProgress * end);
```
**Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM**
- Simple mathematical expression
- Called via requestAnimationFrame
- Execution time: <100μs per frame
- JS is more than sufficient

### 3. Conclusion

**No operations in this codebase warrant WASM optimization.**

Reasons:
1. **Scale too small**: Operations process 4-400 items, not millions
2. **Overhead exceeds benefit**: WASM boundary crossing costs more than the computation
3. **Already optimized**: Heavy operations (Three.js rendering) are already GPU-accelerated
4. **Complexity cost**: WASM adds build toolchain, maintenance burden

### 4. When WASM WOULD Be Appropriate
For reference, WASM makes sense for:
- Image processing (thousands of pixels)
- Real-time audio processing
- Physics simulations with many objects
- Cryptographic operations
- Data compression/decompression

## Recommendation
Focus optimization efforts on React rendering patterns (useMemo, React.memo) rather than WASM. The performance bottlenecks in this site are render cycles, not computation.

## Future Consideration
If the site ever includes:
- Client-side markdown rendering of very long documents
- Complex data visualizations with thousands of points
- Real-time collaborative editing

Then WASM should be re-evaluated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:50:25.783888310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:34.624118446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:34.624118446Z","close_reason":"No WASM candidates found. parseStatValue (<1μs), Fuse.js search (~2ms), star generation (~5ms) - all too fast for WASM overhead to provide benefit. Focus shifted to React rendering optimizations.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cujd","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-7ej3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:52:57.468589007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cw4","title":"Add cass_memory_system to content.ts (152 stars)","description":"## What
Add cass_memory_system project to the projects array in jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts

## Project Details
- **Name**: cass_memory_system (or "CASS Memory System")
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system
- **Stars**: 152
- **Description**: No GitHub description currently - need to research

## Background & Context
- CASS = Coding Agent Session Search (likely related to coding_agent_session_search)
- This appears to be a memory/persistence layer for the CASS ecosystem
- Part of Jeffrey's Flywheel tooling for AI coding agents
- Research code at /data/projects/cass_memory_system/ to understand actual purpose

## Implementation
Research the codebase first, then add:
```typescript
{
  name: "CASS Memory System",
  kind: "oss",
  badge: "152 stars",
  href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system",
  short: "<determined from research>",
  description: "<determined from research>",
  tags: ["Memory", "Agents", "Search", "<other relevant tags>"],
}
```

## Research Questions
- What does CASS stand for in this context?
- How does it relate to coding_agent_session_search?
- What memory/persistence capabilities does it provide?
- What language is it written in?

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project appears in projects array
- Description accurately reflects actual functionality
- Relationship to other CASS tools is clear if relevant","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:23:51.537101434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:11:52.232950422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:11:52.232950422Z","close_reason":"CASS Memory System already exists - updated star count from 60 to 152","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-d20","title":"Bug: Star count formatting inconsistent (should use K/M format)","description":"# Star Count Formatting Inconsistency

## The Problem
Tool cards display star counts using `toLocaleString()` which produces "1,400" but the site-wide pattern (used in hero stats, other pages) uses K/M format like "1.4K" or "3.6K+".

## Current Behavior
- `tldr-tool-card.tsx:237`: `{tool.stars.toLocaleString()}` → "1,400"
- Hero stats show "3,600+" (hardcoded string, not calculated)
- Creates visual inconsistency and makes numbers harder to scan

## Expected Behavior
- Star counts should display as "1.4K", "891", "132" etc.
- Numbers under 1000 show as-is
- Numbers ≥1000 show with K suffix (one decimal if needed)
- Numbers ≥1000000 show with M suffix

## Files to Modify
- `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Line 237, star display
- Consider creating shared util in `lib/utils.ts` for `formatStarCount()`

## Implementation Notes
- Site already has `formatNumber()` in `lib/github-stats.ts` - consider reusing or unifying
- Pattern: `num >= 1000 ? (num / 1000).toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/, '') + 'K' : num.toString()`
- Ensure screen readers get full number via aria-label

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Star counts display in K/M format consistently
- [ ] Numbers under 1000 display as-is (no decimal)
- [ ] Aria-label contains full number for accessibility
- [ ] Visual consistency with rest of site","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:50:06.691901045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T00:26:05.223756529Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T00:26:05.223756529Z","close_reason":"Fixed star count formatting in tldr-tool-card.tsx to use formatStarCount() for K/M format, added accessibility labels with full numbers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-d20","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ktq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-13T00:18:30.845617847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
